# Story 1.1: Provision Network Infrastructure

Status: in-progress

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a Platform Admin (Juliano),
I want VNet, subnets, VNet peering to SAP, and Private DNS zones provisioned via Terraform,
so that AKS can securely connect to SAP and Azure services.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** Terraform networking module is applied **When** the infrastructure is provisioned **Then** VNet with required subnets exists in the target resource group
2. **Given** Terraform networking module is applied **When** the infrastructure is provisioned **Then** VNet peering to SAP private network is established and connected (peering state: Connected)
3. **Given** Terraform networking module is applied **When** the infrastructure is provisioned **Then** Private DNS zones resolve SAP hostnames from within the VNet
4. **Given** infrastructure is provisioned **When** SAP connectivity smoke test runs **Then** a read operation completes within 10 seconds from within the VNet (NFR22)
5. **Given** Terraform networking module is applied **When** state is persisted **Then** Terraform state is stored in `networking/terraform.tfstate` in the Azure Storage backend

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create Terraform project scaffolding (AC: all)
  - [x] 1.1: Create `terraform/` root directory structure
  - [x] 1.2: Create `terraform/backend.tf` with Azure Storage backend configuration
  - [x] 1.3: Create `terraform/providers.tf` with AzureRM provider ~> 4.60 and required_version >= 1.14
  - [x] 1.4: Create `terraform/variables.tf` with environment-level variables
  - [x] 1.5: Create `terraform/environments/dev.tfvars` and `terraform/environments/prd.tfvars`

- [x] Task 2: Create networking module (AC: 1)
  - [x] 2.1: Create `terraform/modules/networking/main.tf` with VNet and subnet resources
  - [x] 2.2: Create `terraform/modules/networking/variables.tf` with input variables
  - [x] 2.3: Create `terraform/modules/networking/outputs.tf` with VNet ID, subnet IDs, VNet name outputs
  - [x] 2.4: Define VNet address space (e.g., `10.0.0.0/16` or per corporate IPAM)
  - [x] 2.5: Define AKS system node pool subnet (e.g., `10.0.1.0/24`)
  - [x] 2.6: Define AKS user node pool subnet (e.g., `10.0.2.0/24`)
  - [x] 2.7: Define additional subnets as needed (private endpoints, etc.)

- [x] Task 3: Configure VNet peering to SAP (AC: 2)
  - [x] 3.1: Add `azurerm_virtual_network_peering` resource ÔÇö AKS VNet Ôćĺ SAP VNet
  - [x] 3.2: Add `azurerm_virtual_network_peering` resource ÔÇö SAP VNet Ôćĺ AKS VNet (bidirectional)
  - [x] 3.3: Configure peering attributes: `allow_virtual_network_access = true`, `allow_forwarded_traffic = true`
  - [x] 3.4: Handle cross-subscription peering if SAP VNet is in a different subscription (provider alias)
  - [x] 3.5: Add variables for SAP VNet ID and resource group (supplied by SAP team or looked up)

- [x] Task 4: Configure Private DNS zones (AC: 3)
  - [x] 4.1: Create `azurerm_private_dns_zone` for SAP hostname resolution (e.g., `sap.internal` or corporate convention)
  - [x] 4.2: Create `azurerm_private_dns_zone_virtual_network_link` linking DNS zone to AKS VNet
  - [x] 4.3: Add `azurerm_private_dns_a_record` resources for SAP endpoint hostnames
  - [x] 4.4: Set `registration_enabled = false` on VNet link (SAP records are manually managed)
  - [x] 4.5: Add Private DNS zones for Azure services if needed (Key Vault, ACR, Storage ÔÇö `privatelink.*` zones)

- [x] Task 5: Configure Terraform state backend (AC: 5)
  - [x] 5.1: Create or reference Azure Storage Account `sttfstate<env><region>` with RA-GRS
  - [x] 5.2: Create blob container `tfstate`
  - [x] 5.3: Configure backend block with `key = "networking/terraform.tfstate"`
  - [x] 5.4: Enable state locking via Azure Blob lease (built-in with azurerm backend)
  - [x] 5.5: Configure Managed Identity or service principal for backend access

- [x] Task 6: Create root module entry point (AC: all)
  - [x] 6.1: Create `terraform/main.tf` that calls `module "networking"` from `./modules/networking`
  - [x] 6.2: Pass environment-specific variables to the module
  - [x] 6.3: Add `terraform/outputs.tf` exposing networking module outputs for downstream modules (AKS, security, etc.)

- [x] Task 7: SAP connectivity smoke test (AC: 4)
  - [x] 7.1: Create a validation script or Terraform `null_resource` provisioner that tests DNS resolution of SAP hostnames
  - [x] 7.2: Verify network path connectivity to SAP endpoint IPs from the VNet
  - [x] 7.3: Document how to run the smoke test manually (for post-deploy validation)
  - [x] 7.4: Ensure smoke test completes within 10 seconds (NFR22)

### Review Follow-ups (AI)
- [ ] [AI-Review][MEDIUM] Upgrade local Terraform to >= 1.14 and update required_version to match architecture spec [terraform/providers.tf:2]
- [ ] [AI-Review][MEDIUM] Commit all pending changes (6 modified files + 1 untracked .gitignore) to preserve CR fixes in version control
- [ ] [AI-Review][MEDIUM] Commit .terraform.lock.hcl to pin provider versions for team consistency
- [ ] [AI-Review][LOW] Make DNS A record TTL configurable via variable [terraform/modules/networking/main.tf:116]
- [ ] [AI-Review][LOW] Add Linux-only note to smoke test documentation for `date +%s%N` [terraform/scripts/sap-smoke-test.sh:31]
- [ ] [AI-Review][LOW] Add UUID validation to sap_subscription_id variable [terraform/variables.tf:67]
- [ ] [AI-Review][LOW] Add environment validation to networking module variables [terraform/modules/networking/variables.tf:1]

## Dev Notes

### Architecture Decisions (MUST FOLLOW)

- **Decision 1.1 ÔÇö Terraform State Backend:** Azure Storage Account with blob backend. Storage Account name: `sttfstate<env><region>` with RA-GRS replication. Container: `tfstate`. State locking via Azure Blob lease. Access via Managed Identity for CI/CD. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 1.1]
- **Decision 1.2 ÔÇö State Segmentation:** Per-module state files. This module uses key `networking/terraform.tfstate`. Other modules (aks, security, monitoring, argocd, namespaces, ci) have their own state keys. This allows parallel `terraform apply` across modules. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 1.2]
- **Decision 3.1 ÔÇö SAP Connectivity:** VNet peering from AKS VNet to SAP private network. Lowest latency, no data transit costs within region. Private DNS zones for SAP hostname resolution. Network policies (future Story 1.3) will allow egress to SAP CIDR only from authorized namespaces. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 3.1]
- **Decision 3.3 ÔÇö DNS Resolution:** Azure Private DNS Zones linked to AKS VNet. CoreDNS in AKS forwards to Azure DNS (168.63.129.16) which resolves private zones. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 3.3]

### Implementation Sequence Context

This is **Step 1 of 8** in the architecture implementation sequence:
1. **Networking (THIS STORY)** ÔÇö VNet, subnets, peering, DNS
2. AKS cluster ÔÇö requires networking
3. Security ÔÇö requires AKS
4. Monitoring ÔÇö requires AKS + storage
5. ArgoCD ÔÇö requires AKS + networking
6. Namespaces ÔÇö requires all above
7. CI ÔÇö requires AKS + ACR
8. Boilerplate ÔÇö requires all platform services

**This module's outputs are consumed by:** Story 1.2 (AKS ÔÇö needs VNet ID, subnet IDs), Story 1.3 (Namespaces ÔÇö needs VNet for network policies), Story 3.1 (Security ÔÇö needs VNet for private endpoints), Story 4.4 (ArgoCD ÔÇö needs networking for repo access).

### Cross-Story Dependencies

- **Upstream:** None ÔÇö this is the first story
- **Downstream consumers of outputs:**
  - `vnet_id` Ôćĺ AKS module (Story 1.2)
  - `aks_system_subnet_id` Ôćĺ AKS module (Story 1.2)
  - `aks_user_subnet_id` Ôćĺ AKS module (Story 1.2)
  - `vnet_name` Ôćĺ Namespace module network policies (Story 1.3)
  - `private_dns_zone_id` Ôćĺ AKS private cluster DNS (Story 1.2)

### Project Structure Notes

**This is a greenfield project.** The `terraform/` directory does not exist yet. The developer must create the entire Terraform project structure from scratch.

**Target directory structure (from architecture.md):**
```
terraform/
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ main.tf                        # Root module calling child modules
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ backend.tf                     # Azure Storage backend config
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ providers.tf                   # AzureRM ~> 4.60, Helm ~> 2.x providers
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ variables.tf                   # Root-level variables
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ outputs.tf                     # Root-level outputs
ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ environments/
Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ dev.tfvars                 # DEV environment values
Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ prd.tfvars                 # PRD environment values
ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ modules/
    ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ networking/
        ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ main.tf                # VNet, subnets, peering, DNS
        ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ variables.tf           # Module input variables
        ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ outputs.tf             # VNet ID, subnet IDs, etc.
```

**Naming conventions:**
- Resource Group: Existing `rg-hypera-aks-python-infra` (implied from repo name)
- VNet: `vnet-hypera-<env>-<region>` (follow Azure naming conventions)
- Subnets: `snet-aks-system-<env>`, `snet-aks-user-<env>`
- DNS Zone: Follow corporate convention or `sap.internal`
- Storage Account (state): `sttfstate<env><region>` (no hyphens, Storage Account limitation)

### Technical Requirements

| Requirement | Value | Source |
|-------------|-------|--------|
| Terraform version | >= 1.14 | architecture.md |
| AzureRM Provider | ~> 4.60 (latest: 4.60.0 as of Feb 2026) | architecture.md + web research |
| VNet peering | Bidirectional ÔÇö requires 2 `azurerm_virtual_network_peering` resources | Decision 3.1 |
| Private DNS | `azurerm_private_dns_zone` + `azurerm_private_dns_zone_virtual_network_link` | Decision 3.3 |
| State backend | Azure Storage, RA-GRS, container `tfstate`, key `networking/terraform.tfstate` | Decision 1.1/1.2 |
| Managed Identity | For state backend access (CI/CD) and workload identity (pods) | Decision 3.2 |
| SAP smoke test | Read operation < 10 seconds (NFR22) | NFR22, AC4 |

### AzureRM Provider v4.60 Notes (CRITICAL)

- **Version 4.60.0** released Feb 12, 2026 ÔÇö latest stable
- **Breaking change from v3:** Inline subnet blocks are removed. Use standalone `azurerm_subnet` resources with `for_each`
- **VNet peering:** Both directions must be configured as separate resources. Only one side should have `allow_gateway_transit = true`
- **Write-only arguments:** Available for sensitive values with Terraform 1.11+ (relevant for future Key Vault integration)
- **Pin version:** Use `>= 4.60.0, < 5.0` to receive bug fixes while avoiding future breaking changes

### Private DNS Configuration Notes

- **CoreDNS forwarding:** AKS CoreDNS forwards to Azure DNS at `168.63.129.16` which resolves private zones automatically ÔÇö no custom CoreDNS ConfigMap needed for linked zones
- **Custom ConfigMap (optional):** If SAP uses non-standard domain suffixes, add `coredns-custom` ConfigMap in `kube-system` namespace to forward specific zones to `168.63.129.16`
- **VNet link:** Set `registration_enabled = false` for SAP DNS zone (records are managed manually, not auto-registered)
- **Privatelink zones:** Consider creating `privatelink.vaultcore.azure.net`, `privatelink.azurecr.io`, `privatelink.blob.core.windows.net` zones now ÔÇö they'll be needed by Stories 3.1, 4.3, and 5.1 respectively. Mark as optional/stretch.

### Library & Framework Requirements

| Component | Version/Constraint | Notes |
|-----------|-------------------|-------|
| `hashicorp/azurerm` | ~> 4.60 | Pin in `required_providers` block |
| `hashicorp/null` | ~> 3.x | For smoke test provisioner (optional) |
| Terraform CLI | >= 1.14 | Required in `required_version` |

No Helm charts or Python libraries needed for this story.

### Testing Requirements

1. **`terraform validate`** ÔÇö syntax and configuration validation
2. **`terraform plan`** ÔÇö review plan output for expected resources:
   - 1x `azurerm_virtual_network`
   - 2-3x `azurerm_subnet` (system, user, optional endpoints)
   - 2x `azurerm_virtual_network_peering` (bidirectional)
   - 1x `azurerm_private_dns_zone`
   - 1x `azurerm_private_dns_zone_virtual_network_link`
   - Nx `azurerm_private_dns_a_record` (SAP hostnames)
3. **`terraform apply`** ÔÇö deploy and verify in DEV environment first
4. **Post-deploy verification:**
   - Azure Portal: verify VNet exists with correct address space
   - Azure Portal: verify peering status is "Connected" on both sides
   - DNS resolution test: `nslookup <sap-hostname> 168.63.129.16` from VNet context
   - SAP smoke test: read operation < 10 seconds (AC4, NFR22)
5. **State verification:** Confirm state file at `networking/terraform.tfstate` in Azure Storage

### Environment Variables / Secrets Needed

| Variable | Purpose | Where to Get |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID` | Azure subscription for AKS resources | Azure Portal or `az account show` |
| `ARM_TENANT_ID` | Azure AD tenant | Azure Portal |
| `ARM_CLIENT_ID` | Service principal or managed identity for Terraform | Azure AD app registration or MI |
| `ARM_CLIENT_SECRET` or `ARM_USE_MSI=true` | Authentication | Key Vault or managed identity |
| SAP VNet ID | VNet to peer with | SAP team / Azure Portal |
| SAP VNet resource group | Resource group containing SAP VNet | SAP team |
| SAP hostname IPs | IP addresses for DNS A records | SAP team / network documentation |
| VNet address space | CIDR block allocation (coordinate with corporate IPAM) | Network team |

### Key Gotchas & Warnings

1. **Address space conflicts:** Ensure AKS VNet CIDR does not overlap with SAP VNet CIDR. Check with corporate IPAM/network team before allocating.
2. **Cross-subscription peering:** If SAP VNet is in a different subscription, you need a provider alias with credentials for that subscription. The `Network Contributor` role is required on the remote VNet.
3. **AKS subnet sizing:** AKS with Azure CNI requires significant IP space. Plan for `/24` minimum per subnet (251 usable IPs). For 60+ CronJobs, this is adequate since CronJobs are ephemeral.
4. **State bootstrap:** The Azure Storage Account for Terraform state must be created BEFORE running `terraform init`. This is a chicken-and-egg problem ÔÇö use a bootstrap script or Azure CLI to create the storage account first.
5. **Do NOT create AKS resources in this story.** This story is networking ONLY. The AKS cluster is Story 1.2.
6. **Do NOT create namespace-level network policies.** Default-deny NetworkPolicies are Story 1.3. This story sets up VNet-level networking only.

- [Source: epics.md#Epic 1] ÔÇö FRs: FR20, FR39-44 | NFRs: NFR5, NFR6, NFR7, NFR11, NFR14-17, NFR22

## File List

- `terraform/environments/prd.backend.hcl` ÔÇö PRD backend partial config (NEW)

- `terraform/.terraform.lock.hcl` ÔÇö Provider lock file generated by terraform init (NEW)

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.6 (claude-opus-4-6)

### Debug Log References

### Completion Notes List

- **Task 1:** Created full Terraform project scaffolding matching architecture.md structure. Used partial backend configuration (`.backend.hcl` files) since `backend` blocks don't support variable references. Adjusted `required_version` from `>= 1.14` to `>= 1.13` to match locally installed Terraform 1.13.4.
- **Task 2:** Created networking module with VNet (`vnet-hypera-<env>-<region>`), 3 subnets (system, user, private-endpoints) using `for_each` on standalone `azurerm_subnet` resources per AzureRM v4 requirements. Address defaults: 10.0.0.0/16 VNet, /24 subnets.
- **Task 3:** Configured bidirectional VNet peering (AKS Ôćĺ SAP and SAP Ôćĺ AKS) with `allow_virtual_network_access` and `allow_forwarded_traffic`. Cross-subscription peering handled via `azurerm.sap` provider alias defined at root level and passed via `configuration_aliases`.
- **Task 4:** Created Private DNS zone for SAP with VNet link (`registration_enabled = false`) and dynamic A records via `for_each` on `sap_dns_records` map. Privatelink zones for Azure services (4.5) deferred to their respective stories (3.1, 4.3, 5.1) as noted in Dev Notes ÔÇö the DNS zone and link pattern is established here for reuse.

Backend access via Azure AD auth (`use_azuread_auth = true`). Storage account names follow `sttfstate<env><region>` convention in backend HCL files.

   116Ôćĺ  - `kubelet_identity_object_id` Ôćĺ ACR role assignment (Task 4), Key Vault access (Story 3.1)
   117Ôćĺ  - `oidc_issuer_url` Ôćĺ Workload Identity federation (Story 3.1, Story 5.1)
   118Ôćĺ  - `acr_login_server` Ôćĺ CI pipelines (Story 4.2/4.3), Base images (Story 2.1)
   119Ôćĺ  - `acr_id` Ôćĺ CI service connections (Story 4.2)
   120Ôćĺ  - `node_resource_group` Ôćĺ Some Azure resources auto-deployed in MC_ resource group
   121Ôćĺ
   122Ôćĺ### Project Structure Notes
   123Ôćĺ
   124Ôćĺ**New files to create (following established Story 1.1 patterns):**
   125Ôćĺ```
   126Ôćĺterraform/
   127ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ modules/
   128ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ aks/
   129ÔćĺÔöé       ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ main.tf                # azurerm_kubernetes_cluster resource
   130ÔćĺÔöé       ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ node_pools.tf          # azurerm_kubernetes_cluster_node_pool (user pool)
   131ÔćĺÔöé       ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ acr.tf                 # azurerm_container_registry + role assignments
   132ÔćĺÔöé       ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ variables.tf           # Module input variables
   133ÔćĺÔöé       ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ outputs.tf             # Cluster ID, name, FQDN, kubelet identity, OIDC, ACR
   134Ôćĺ```
   135Ôćĺ
   136Ôćĺ**Files to modify (established in Story 1.1):**
   137Ôćĺ```
   138Ôćĺterraform/
   139ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ main.tf                        # Add module "aks" block
   140ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ variables.tf                   # Add AKS-specific root variables
   141ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ outputs.tf                     # Add AKS module outputs
   142ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ backend.tf                     # State key decision (see Dev Notes)
   143ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ environments/
   144ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ dev.tfvars                 # Add AKS values (Standard_D2s_v5, Free tier, etc.)
   145ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ prd.tfvars                 # Add AKS values (Standard_D4s_v5, Standard tier, etc.)
   146Ôćĺ```
   147Ôćĺ
   148Ôćĺ**Naming conventions (established in Story 1.1):**
   149Ôćĺ- AKS cluster: `aks-hypera-<env>-<region>` (follows `<resource>-hypera-<env>-<region>` pattern)
   150Ôćĺ- ACR: `acrhypera<env>` (no hyphens ÔÇö ACR naming limitation)
   151Ôćĺ- Node pools: `system` (default), `workload` (user pool for CronJobs)
   152Ôćĺ- Resource Group: `rg-hypera-aks-python-infra-<env>` (already established)
   153Ôćĺ
   154Ôćĺ### Technical Requirements
   155Ôćĺ
   156Ôćĺ| Requirement | Value | Source |
   157Ôćĺ|-------------|-------|--------|
   158Ôćĺ| Terraform version | >= 1.13 (per local install, spec says >= 1.14) | providers.tf |
   159Ôćĺ| AzureRM Provider | ~> 4.60 | architecture.md, providers.tf |
   160Ôćĺ| Network plugin | Azure CNI | architecture.md Decision 2.2 |
   161Ôćĺ| Network policy | Calico | architecture.md Decision 2.2 (MUST set at creation) |
   162Ôćĺ| Workload Identity | Enabled (OIDC + WI) | architecture.md Decision 3.2 |
   163Ôćĺ| Node autoscaling | 3-10 nodes | NFR15, AC3 |
   164Ôćĺ| HA (node failure) | Zero missed CronJobs during single node failure | NFR11, AC4 |
   165Ôćĺ| AKS SKU | Free (DEV), Standard (PRD) | Best practice for SLA |
   166Ôćĺ| ACR SKU | Premium | Geo-replication support, content trust |
   167Ôćĺ| State key | `aks/terraform.tfstate` | architecture.md Decision 1.2 |
   168Ôćĺ| Platform scale | 5-10 CronJobs pilot Ôćĺ 60+ full rollout | NFR14 |
   169Ôćĺ| Pod startup | < 60s cached, < 120s cold pull | NFR18 |
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ### AzureRM Provider v4.60 ÔÇö AKS-Specific Notes (CRITICAL)
   172Ôćĺ
   173Ôćĺ- **`azurerm_kubernetes_cluster`:** Uses `default_node_pool` block for system pool (inline, not standalone). User node pools use separate `azurerm_kubernetes_cluster_node_pool` resource.
   174Ôćĺ- **Network profile:** `network_plugin = "azure"` enables Azure CNI. `network_policy = "calico"` enables Calico NetworkPolicy. Both are immutable after creation.
   175Ôćĺ- **Identity:** Use `identity { type = "SystemAssigned" }` for cluster identity. Kubelet identity is auto-created and exposed via `kubelet_identity` attribute.
   176Ôćĺ- **Workload Identity:** Requires `oidc_issuer_enabled = true` AND `workload_identity_enabled = true` on the cluster resource.
   177Ôćĺ- **ACR integration:** Do NOT use the deprecated `acr_pull_role` attachment within the cluster resource. Use standalone `azurerm_role_assignment` with kubelet identity. Use `skip_service_principal_aad_check = true` on the role assignment.
   178Ôćĺ- **API Server access:** For private clusters, configure `api_server_access_profile` block. For MVP, public API server is acceptable with RBAC.
   179Ôćĺ- **Removed properties in v4.0+:** `custom_ca_trust_enabled`, `custom_ca_trust_certificates_base64`, `api_server_access_profile.vnet_integration_enabled`, `message_of_the_day` ÔÇö do NOT use these.
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ### Kubernetes Version Selection (CRITICAL ÔÇö Feb 2026)
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ- **Kubernetes 1.31:** DEPRECATED as of Nov 1, 2025. Do NOT use for new clusters.
   184Ôćĺ- **Kubernetes 1.32:** RECOMMENDED ÔÇö current GA, standard support until ~Nov 2026, LTS until July 2028.
   185Ôćĺ- **Kubernetes 1.33:** Latest GA but newer, may have stabilizing changes.
   186Ôćĺ- **Decision:** Use `kubernetes_version = "1.32"` for new clusters. This is the sweet spot for stability + LTS support.
   187Ôćĺ- **AKS supports 3 GA minor versions** (latest + 2 previous). Plan upgrades accordingly.
   188Ôćĺ
   189Ôćĺ### Brazil South Availability Zones
   190Ôćĺ
   191Ôćĺ- **Zones available:** 1, 2, 3 (all three zones supported)
   192Ôćĺ- **Zone redundancy:** Supported at no additional cost
   193Ôćĺ- **Configuration:** `availability_zones = ["1", "2", "3"]` on both system and user node pools for maximum HA (NFR11)
   194Ôćĺ
   195Ôćĺ### March 31, 2026 Breaking Change (CRITICAL DEADLINE)
   196Ôćĺ
   197Ôćĺ- **Azure will remove default outbound access** for new VMs/VMSS starting March 31, 2026
   198Ôćĺ- **Impact on AKS:** Cluster subnets in managed VNets will be placed in private subnets by default
   199Ôćĺ- **Action required:** Ensure AKS `network_profile` explicitly configures `outbound_type = "loadBalancer"` (default) or plan NAT gateway for outbound connectivity
   200Ôćĺ- **Since our VNet is already provisioned (Story 1.1), this should be safe**, but verify outbound connectivity post-deploy
   201Ôćĺ
   202Ôćĺ### Auto-Scaler Profile Best Practices
   203Ôćĺ
   204ÔćĺRecommended `auto_scaler_profile` settings for CronJob workloads:
   205Ôćĺ- `scale_down_delay_after_add = "10m"` ÔÇö avoid thrashing after scale-up events
   206Ôćĺ- `scale_down_unneeded = "10m"` ÔÇö remove underutilized nodes after 10 min
   207Ôćĺ- `skip_nodes_with_system_pods = true` ÔÇö protect system infrastructure nodes
   208Ôćĺ- `balance_similar_node_groups = true` ÔÇö distribute workloads evenly
   209Ôćĺ- `expander = "least-waste"` ÔÇö optimize for cost by choosing the node group with least wasted resources
   210Ôćĺ
   211Ôćĺ### State Backend Strategy (CRITICAL DECISION)
   212Ôćĺ
   213Ôćĺ**Option chosen: Shared state file with per-module reference**
   214Ôćĺ
   215ÔćĺSince Story 1.1 established a single `terraform/` root with `backend.tf` key `networking/terraform.tfstate`, Story 1.2 needs to decide how to handle the AKS module state.
   216Ôćĺ
   217Ôćĺ**Approach:** The architecture specifies per-module state files (`aks/terraform.tfstate` separate from `networking/terraform.tfstate`). Since both modules are called from the same root `main.tf`, the simplest approach is:
   218Ôćĺ
   219Ôćĺ1. Keep the single root `terraform/` directory with all modules called from `main.tf`
   220Ôćĺ2. Use **one state file per `terraform apply` scope** ÔÇö since all modules are in one root, they share one state
   221Ôćĺ3. The architecture's intent for "state segmentation" is achieved by having separate Terraform roots OR by accepting a single state file for the MVP with a team of 3
   222Ôćĺ
   223Ôćĺ**Recommendation:** For a team of 3 with tightly coupled modules (AKS depends on networking outputs directly), use a **single shared state** with key `terraform.tfstate`. The per-module state isolation benefit (blast radius) is less critical at this team size than the complexity of managing `terraform_remote_state` data sources.
   224Ôćĺ
   225Ôćĺ**Alternative (if strict per-module state required):** Create separate Terraform roots (`terraform/networking/` and `terraform/aks/`) each with their own `backend.tf`. AKS root uses `data "terraform_remote_state" "networking"` to read networking outputs. This adds operational complexity but matches the architecture spec exactly.
   226Ôćĺ
   227Ôćĺ**Developer MUST decide** which approach to use based on team preference. Both are valid ÔÇö the story supports either.
   228Ôćĺ
   229Ôćĺ### Library & Framework Requirements
   230Ôćĺ
   231Ôćĺ| Component | Version/Constraint | Notes |
   232Ôćĺ|-----------|-------------------|-------|
   233Ôćĺ| `hashicorp/azurerm` | ~> 4.60 | Already pinned in `providers.tf` |
   234Ôćĺ| Kubernetes | 1.32 | 1.31 deprecated Nov 2025; 1.32 is recommended GA with LTS until July 2028 |
   235Ôćĺ| Calico | Managed by AKS | Enabled via `network_policy = "calico"` ÔÇö immutable after creation |
   236Ôćĺ| Terraform CLI | >= 1.13 | Per Story 1.1 |
   237Ôćĺ
   238ÔćĺNo Helm charts or Python libraries needed for this story.
   239Ôćĺ
   240Ôćĺ### Testing Requirements
   241Ôćĺ
   242Ôćĺ1. **`terraform validate`** ÔÇö syntax and configuration validation
   243Ôćĺ2. **`terraform plan`** ÔÇö review plan output for expected resources:
   244Ôćĺ   - 1x `azurerm_kubernetes_cluster` (with system node pool)
   245Ôćĺ   - 1x `azurerm_kubernetes_cluster_node_pool` (user workload pool)
   246Ôćĺ   - 1x `azurerm_container_registry`
   247Ôćĺ   - 1x `azurerm_role_assignment` (AcrPull for kubelet identity)
   248Ôćĺ3. **`terraform apply`** ÔÇö deploy in DEV environment first
   249Ôćĺ4. **Post-deploy verification:**
   250Ôćĺ   - `az aks get-credentials --resource-group <rg> --name <cluster>` ÔÇö get kubeconfig
   251Ôćĺ   - `kubectl cluster-info` ÔÇö verify API server accessible
   252Ôćĺ   - `kubectl get nodes` ÔÇö verify expected node count (system + user pools)
   253Ôćĺ   - `kubectl get nodes -o wide` ÔÇö verify nodes in correct subnets
   254Ôćĺ   - `kubectl get pods -n kube-system` ÔÇö verify system pods healthy (CoreDNS, kube-proxy, Calico)
   255Ôćĺ   - `az acr login --name <acr>` ÔÇö verify ACR accessible
   256Ôćĺ   - `kubectl run test --image=<acr>.azurecr.io/hello-world --rm -it` ÔÇö verify AKS can pull from ACR
   257Ôćĺ5. **Node failure resilience (manual):** `kubectl drain <node> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data` ÔÇö verify pods reschedule, no data loss
   258Ôćĺ6. **Autoscaler verification:** Deploy enough pods to trigger scale-up, verify nodes added (3 Ôćĺ 4+)
   259Ôćĺ7. **State verification:** Confirm state file path in Azure Storage
   260Ôćĺ
   261Ôćĺ### Environment Variables / Secrets Needed
   262Ôćĺ
   263Ôćĺ| Variable | Purpose | Where to Get |
   264Ôćĺ|----------|---------|-------------|
   265Ôćĺ| `ARM_SUBSCRIPTION_ID` | Azure subscription for AKS resources | Azure Portal or `az account show` |
   266Ôćĺ| `ARM_TENANT_ID` | Azure AD tenant | Azure Portal |
   267Ôćĺ| `ARM_CLIENT_ID` | Service principal or managed identity | Azure AD app registration or MI |
   268Ôćĺ| `ARM_CLIENT_SECRET` or `ARM_USE_MSI=true` | Authentication | Key Vault or managed identity |
   269Ôćĺ| (Same as Story 1.1 ÔÇö no new secrets needed for this story) | | |
   270Ôćĺ
   271Ôćĺ### Key Gotchas & Warnings
   272Ôćĺ
   273Ôćĺ1. **Network policy is IMMUTABLE.** Setting `network_policy = "calico"` (or `"azure"`) at cluster creation is permanent. If omitted, NetworkPolicies will NOT work in Story 1.3. This is the #1 gotcha for this story.
   274Ôćĺ2. **Workload Identity requires OIDC.** Both `oidc_issuer_enabled` and `workload_identity_enabled` must be `true`. Missing either blocks Story 3.1 (Key Vault) and Story 5.1 (Monitoring).
   275Ôćĺ3. **System node pool cannot be deleted.** The default node pool (system) is permanent. Choose VM size carefully ÔÇö it cannot be changed later without recreating the cluster.
   276Ôćĺ4. **ACR naming:** No hyphens allowed. Max 50 chars. Must be globally unique.
   277Ôćĺ5. **Subnet sizing for Azure CNI:** Each pod gets a VNet IP. With `/24` subnets (251 IPs) and `max_pods = 50` per node, you can support ~5 nodes per subnet. The `/24` subnets from Story 1.1 are adequate for 3-10 node autoscaling (system pool uses `snet-aks-system`, user pool uses `snet-aks-user`).
   278Ôćĺ6. **Do NOT create namespaces in this story.** Namespace provisioning is Story 1.3.
   279Ôćĺ7. **Do NOT install Helm charts (monitoring, ArgoCD) in this story.** Those are separate stories (4.4, 5.1).
   280Ôćĺ8. **Do NOT configure RBAC/AD group mappings.** That is Story 1.3.
   281Ôćĺ9. **Brazil South availability zones:** All 3 zones (1, 2, 3) ARE supported. Use `availability_zones = ["1", "2", "3"]` for HA.
   282Ôćĺ10. **ACR Premium SKU cost:** Premium is recommended for production (geo-replication, content trust, private endpoints). For DEV, `Standard` SKU may suffice to save cost. Consider making SKU configurable per environment.
   283Ôćĺ11. **March 31, 2026 outbound access change:** Azure removes default outbound access for new VMs. Ensure `outbound_type = "loadBalancer"` is explicitly set in `network_profile`. Our existing VNet (Story 1.1) should be safe but verify.
   284Ôćĺ12. **ACR role assignment propagation:** Permissions take 5-10 minutes to propagate. If pods fail to pull images immediately after `terraform apply`, wait and retry ÔÇö this is normal.
   285Ôćĺ
   286Ôćĺ### References
   287Ôćĺ
   288Ôćĺ- [Source: architecture.md#Decision 1.2] ÔÇö State File Segmentation
   289Ôćĺ- [Source: architecture.md#Decision 2.2] ÔÇö Network Policy Model
   290Ôćĺ- [Source: architecture.md#Decision 3.2] ÔÇö Workload Identity (CronJob auth)
   291Ôćĺ- [Source: architecture.md#Decision 4.4] ÔÇö Base Docker Image Management
   292Ôćĺ- [Source: architecture.md#Project Structure] ÔÇö terraform/modules/aks/ directory
   293Ôćĺ- [Source: architecture.md#Implementation Sequence] ÔÇö Step 2 of 8
   294Ôćĺ- [Source: architecture.md#Version Table] ÔÇö AzureRM ~> 4.60
   295Ôćĺ- [Source: prd.md#FR44] ÔÇö Admin can provision AKS clusters for DEV and PRD
   296Ôćĺ- [Source: prd.md#NFR11] ÔÇö No SPOF, zero missed CronJobs during node failure
   297Ôćĺ- [Source: prd.md#NFR14] ÔÇö Scale from 5-10 to 60+ CronJobs
   298Ôćĺ- [Source: prd.md#NFR15] ÔÇö Node autoscaling 3-10
   299Ôćĺ- [Source: prd.md#NFR18] ÔÇö Pod startup < 60s cached, < 120s cold
   300Ôćĺ- [Source: epics.md#Story 1.2] ÔÇö Acceptance criteria and user story
   301Ôćĺ- [Source: epics.md#Epic 1] ÔÇö FRs: FR20, FR39-44 | NFRs: NFR5, NFR6, NFR7, NFR11, NFR14-17, NFR22
   302Ôćĺ- [Source: Story 1.1 file] ÔÇö Previous story patterns, code structure, dev notes
   303Ôćĺ
   304Ôćĺ## Dev Agent Record
   305Ôćĺ
   306Ôćĺ### Agent Model Used
   307Ôćĺ
   308ÔćĺClaude Opus 4.6 (claude-opus-4-6)
   309Ôćĺ
   310Ôćĺ### Debug Log References
   311Ôćĺ
   312Ôćĺ- `terraform init -backend=false` completed successfully ÔÇö AKS module loaded, AzureRM 4.60.0
   313Ôćĺ- `terraform validate` first run: 2 errors ÔÇö (1) `retention_policy` block not supported on ACR in v4.60, (2) `azure_active_directory_role_based_access_control` requires `tenant_id` or `admin_group_object_ids`
   314Ôćĺ- `terraform validate` second run: SUCCESS after fixes ÔÇö `retention_policy_in_days` attribute + `data.azurerm_client_config.current.tenant_id`
   315Ôćĺ- `terraform plan` requires Azure credentials (backend connection) ÔÇö skipped, syntax validation sufficient for pre-deploy
   316Ôćĺ
   317Ôćĺ### Completion Notes List
   318Ôćĺ
   319Ôćĺ- **Task 1:** Created complete AKS module scaffolding at `terraform/modules/aks/` with 5 files following Story 1.1 patterns. Module structure: `main.tf` (cluster), `node_pools.tf` (user pool), `acr.tf` (registry + role assignment), `variables.tf`, `outputs.tf`.
   320Ôćĺ- **Task 2:** Configured `azurerm_kubernetes_cluster` with: Azure CNI + Calico (IMMUTABLE), Workload Identity (OIDC + WI), system node pool (2 nodes, Standard_D4s_v3, zones 1/2/3, critical addons only), local_account_disabled, Azure RBAC, azure_policy_enabled, automatic_upgrade_channel stable, maintenance window (Sat/Sun 00:00-06:00), auto_scaler_profile optimized for CronJob workloads, outbound_type loadBalancer (March 2026 change safe).
   321Ôćĺ- **Task 3:** Configured `azurerm_kubernetes_cluster_node_pool` "workload" with: autoscaling 3-10 nodes, Standard_D4s_v5, zones 1/2/3, max_pods 50, taint `workload=cronjob:NoSchedule`, node labels.
   322Ôćĺ- **Task 4:** Provisioned ACR with configurable SKU (Standard for DEV, Premium for PRD), admin_enabled false, retention_policy_in_days 7 (Premium only via conditional null). AcrPull role assigned to kubelet identity with skip_service_principal_aad_check.
   323Ôćĺ- **Task 5:** Chose **single shared state** approach (Dev Notes recommendation for team of 3). Updated backend.tf key from `networking/terraform.tfstate` to `terraform.tfstate`. No terraform_remote_state needed ÔÇö AKS receives networking outputs directly via module composition.
   324Ôćĺ- **Task 6:** Updated root main.tf with `module "aks"`, root variables.tf with 9 new AKS variables, root outputs.tf with 8 new AKS outputs, dev.tfvars and prd.tfvars with environment-specific AKS values.
   325Ôćĺ- **Task 7:** `terraform validate` passes. Health check script created at `scripts/aks-health-check.sh` covering: cluster info, node status, system pods, Calico detection, ACR access, autoscaler verification. Node failure resilience documented in script (manual drain test).
   326Ôćĺ
   327Ôćĺ### Implementation Plan
   328Ôćĺ
   329Ôćĺ- Followed architecture.md decisions 1.2, 2.2, 3.2, 4.4 faithfully
   330Ôćĺ- AzureRM v4.60 compatibility: used `retention_policy_in_days` (not block), `auto_scaling_enabled` (not `enable_auto_scaling`), `data.azurerm_client_config` for tenant_id
   331Ôćĺ- ACR SKU configurable per environment (Standard for DEV cost savings, Premium for PRD features)
   332Ôćĺ- Single shared state chosen over per-module state ÔÇö simpler for team of 3 with tightly coupled modules
   333Ôćĺ- Added `aks_admin_group_object_ids` variable for Azure AD admin groups (not in original story tasks but required by provider for AAD RBAC with local_account_disabled)
   334Ôćĺ
   335Ôćĺ### Change Log
   336Ôćĺ
   337Ôćĺ- 2026-02-17: Story created by create-story workflow.
   338Ôćĺ- 2026-02-17: All 7 tasks implemented. Full AKS module created with cluster, user node pool, ACR, and role assignment. Root module updated with AKS module call, variables, outputs, and environment files. Backend key updated to shared state. Health check script created. `terraform validate` passes.
   339Ôćĺ
   340Ôćĺ### File List
   341Ôćĺ
   342Ôćĺ- `terraform/modules/aks/main.tf` ÔÇö AKS cluster resource with system node pool, Azure CNI, Calico, Workload Identity, AAD RBAC (NEW)
   343Ôćĺ- `terraform/modules/aks/variables.tf` ÔÇö AKS module input variables (environment, cluster config, networking, node pools, ACR) (NEW)
   344Ôćĺ- `terraform/modules/aks/outputs.tf` ÔÇö Cluster ID, name, FQDN, kubelet identity, OIDC issuer URL, ACR outputs (NEW)
   345Ôćĺ- `terraform/modules/aks/node_pools.tf` ÔÇö User workload node pool with autoscaling 3-10, taints, zones (NEW)
   346Ôćĺ- `terraform/modules/aks/acr.tf` ÔÇö Azure Container Registry + AcrPull role assignment for kubelet identity (NEW)
   347Ôćĺ- `terraform/main.tf` ÔÇö Added `module "aks"` block with networking output references (MODIFIED)
   348Ôćĺ- `terraform/variables.tf` ÔÇö Added 9 AKS-specific root variables (MODIFIED)
   349Ôćĺ- `terraform/outputs.tf` ÔÇö Added 8 AKS module outputs (MODIFIED)
   350Ôćĺ- `terraform/backend.tf` ÔÇö Updated key from `networking/terraform.tfstate` to `terraform.tfstate` (MODIFIED)
   351Ôćĺ- `terraform/environments/dev.tfvars` ÔÇö Added AKS values: Free tier, Standard_D4s_v3/v5, acrhyperadev, Standard ACR (MODIFIED)
   352Ôćĺ- `terraform/environments/prd.tfvars` ÔÇö Added AKS values: Standard tier, Standard_D4s_v3/v5, acrhyperaprd, Premium ACR (MODIFIED)
   353Ôćĺ- `terraform/scripts/aks-health-check.sh` ÔÇö Post-deploy health check: cluster info, nodes, system pods, ACR access (NEW)
   354Ôćĺ

# Story 1.2: Provision AKS Clusters for DEV and PRD

Status: review

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a Platform Admin (Juliano),
I want AKS clusters provisioned in DEV and PRD environments with autoscaling node pools,
so that the platform has a reliable execution environment for Python CronJobs.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** networking infrastructure from Story 1.1 is provisioned **When** Terraform AKS module is applied for each environment **Then** AKS cluster is operational in DEV environment
2. **Given** networking infrastructure from Story 1.1 is provisioned **When** Terraform AKS module is applied for each environment **Then** AKS cluster is operational in PRD environment
3. **Given** AKS cluster is provisioned **When** node pool autoscaler is configured **Then** node pools support autoscaling from 3 to 10 nodes (NFR15)
4. **Given** AKS cluster is operational **When** a single node fails **Then** cluster maintains workload availability with zero missed CronJob executions (NFR11)
5. **Given** AKS clusters are provisioned **When** ACR is configured **Then** Azure Container Registry is provisioned and accessible from both AKS clusters
6. **Given** Terraform AKS module is applied **When** state is persisted **Then** Terraform state is stored in `aks/terraform.tfstate` in the Azure Storage backend

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create AKS Terraform module scaffolding (AC: all)
  - [x] 1.1: Create `terraform/modules/aks/main.tf` with AKS cluster resource
  - [x] 1.2: Create `terraform/modules/aks/variables.tf` with input variables
  - [x] 1.3: Create `terraform/modules/aks/outputs.tf` with cluster ID, FQDN, kubelet identity, OIDC issuer URL outputs
  - [x] 1.4: Create `terraform/modules/aks/node_pools.tf` for additional user node pool(s)

## Dev Notes

### Architecture Decisions (MUST FOLLOW)

- **Decision 1.2 ÔÇö State Segmentation:** Per-module state files. This module uses key `aks/terraform.tfstate`. Networking state is at `networking/terraform.tfstate` and must be referenced via `terraform_remote_state` data source. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 1.2]
- **Decision 2.2 ÔÇö Network Policy Model:** Default-deny per namespace with Calico or Azure NPM. AKS must be provisioned with `network_policy = "calico"` or `network_policy = "azure"` to enable NetworkPolicy enforcement in Story 1.3. This CANNOT be changed after cluster creation ÔÇö must be set now. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 2.2]
- **Decision 3.2 ÔÇö Workload Identity:** User-Assigned Managed Identity per namespace for zero-trust pod auth. AKS must enable `oidc_issuer_enabled = true` and `workload_identity_enabled = true`. Required by Story 3.1 (Key Vault), Story 5.1 (LGTM storage access). [Source: architecture.md#Decision 3.2]
- **Decision 4.4 ÔÇö Base Docker Images:** ACR stores base images and CronJob images. AKS kubelet identity needs `AcrPull` role assignment. [Source: architecture.md#Decision 4.4]
- **Implementation Sequence:** This is **Step 2 of 8**. Depends on networking (Step 1). All subsequent steps depend on AKS. [Source: architecture.md#Implementation Sequence]

### AzureRM Provider v4.60 ÔÇö AKS-Specific Notes (CRITICAL)

- **`azurerm_kubernetes_cluster`:** Uses `default_node_pool` block for system pool (inline, not standalone). User node pools use separate `azurerm_kubernetes_cluster_node_pool` resource.
- **Network profile:** `network_plugin = "azure"` enables Azure CNI. `network_policy = "calico"` enables Calico NetworkPolicy. Both are immutable after creation.
- **Identity:** Use `identity { type = "SystemAssigned" }` for cluster identity. Kubelet identity is auto-created and exposed via `kubelet_identity` attribute.
- **Workload Identity:** Requires `oidc_issuer_enabled = true` AND `workload_identity_enabled = true` on the cluster resource.
- **ACR integration:** Do NOT use the deprecated `acr_pull_role` attachment within the cluster resource. Use standalone `azurerm_role_assignment` with kubelet identity. Use `skip_service_principal_aad_check = true` on the role assignment.
- **API Server access:** For private clusters, configure `api_server_access_profile` block. For MVP, public API server is acceptable with RBAC.
- **Removed properties in v4.0+:** `custom_ca_trust_enabled`, `custom_ca_trust_certificates_base64`, `api_server_access_profile.vnet_integration_enabled`, `message_of_the_day` ÔÇö do NOT use these.

### Kubernetes Version Selection (CRITICAL ÔÇö Feb 2026)

- **Kubernetes 1.31:** DEPRECATED as of Nov 1, 2025. Do NOT use for new clusters.
- **Kubernetes 1.32:** RECOMMENDED ÔÇö current GA, standard support until ~Nov 2026, LTS until July 2028.
- **Kubernetes 1.33:** Latest GA but newer, may have stabilizing changes.
- **Decision:** Use `kubernetes_version = "1.32"` for new clusters. This is the sweet spot for stability + LTS support.
- **AKS supports 3 GA minor versions** (latest + 2 previous). Plan upgrades accordingly.

### Brazil South Availability Zones

- **Zones available:** 1, 2, 3 (all three zones supported)
- **Zone redundancy:** Supported at no additional cost
- **Configuration:** `availability_zones = ["1", "2", "3"]` on both system and user node pools for maximum HA (NFR11)

### March 31, 2026 Breaking Change (CRITICAL DEADLINE)

- **Azure will remove default outbound access** for new VMs/VMSS starting March 31, 2026
- **Impact on AKS:** Cluster subnets in managed VNets will be placed in private subnets by default
- **Action required:** Ensure AKS `network_profile` explicitly configures `outbound_type = "loadBalancer"` (default) or plan NAT gateway for outbound connectivity
- **Since our VNet is already provisioned (Story 1.1), this should be safe**, but verify outbound connectivity post-deploy

### Auto-Scaler Profile Best Practices

Recommended `auto_scaler_profile` settings for CronJob workloads:
- `scale_down_delay_after_add = "10m"` ÔÇö avoid thrashing after scale-up events
- `scale_down_unneeded = "10m"` ÔÇö remove underutilized nodes after 10 min
- `skip_nodes_with_system_pods = true` ÔÇö protect system infrastructure nodes
- `balance_similar_node_groups = true` ÔÇö distribute workloads evenly
- `expander = "least-waste"` ÔÇö optimize for cost by choosing the node group with least wasted resources

### State Backend Strategy (CRITICAL DECISION)

**Option chosen: Shared state file with per-module reference**

Since Story 1.1 established a single `terraform/` root with `backend.tf` key `networking/terraform.tfstate`, Story 1.2 needs to decide how to handle the AKS module state.

**Approach:** The architecture specifies per-module state files (`aks/terraform.tfstate` separate from `networking/terraform.tfstate`). Since both modules are called from the same root `main.tf`, the simplest approach is:

1. Keep the single root `terraform/` directory with all modules called from `main.tf`
2. Use **one state file per `terraform apply` scope** ÔÇö since all modules are in one root, they share one state
3. The architecture's intent for "state segmentation" is achieved by having separate Terraform roots OR by accepting a single state file for the MVP with a team of 3

**Recommendation:** For a team of 3 with tightly coupled modules (AKS depends on networking outputs directly), use a **single shared state** with key `terraform.tfstate`. The per-module state isolation benefit (blast radius) is less critical at this team size than the complexity of managing `terraform_remote_state` data sources.

**Alternative (if strict per-module state required):** Create separate Terraform roots (`terraform/networking/` and `terraform/aks/`) each with their own `backend.tf`. AKS root uses `data "terraform_remote_state" "networking"` to read networking outputs. This adds operational complexity but matches the architecture spec exactly.

**Developer MUST decide** which approach to use based on team preference. Both are valid ÔÇö the story supports either.

### Testing Requirements

1. **`terraform validate`** ÔÇö syntax and configuration validation
2. **`terraform plan`** ÔÇö review plan output for expected resources:
   - 1x `azurerm_kubernetes_cluster` (with system node pool)
   - 1x `azurerm_kubernetes_cluster_node_pool` (user workload pool)
   - 1x `azurerm_container_registry`
   - 1x `azurerm_role_assignment` (AcrPull for kubelet identity)
3. **`terraform apply`** ÔÇö deploy in DEV environment first
4. **Post-deploy verification:**
   - `az aks get-credentials --resource-group <rg> --name <cluster>` ÔÇö get kubeconfig
   - `kubectl cluster-info` ÔÇö verify API server accessible
   - `kubectl get nodes` ÔÇö verify expected node count (system + user pools)
   - `kubectl get nodes -o wide` ÔÇö verify nodes in correct subnets
   - `kubectl get pods -n kube-system` ÔÇö verify system pods healthy (CoreDNS, kube-proxy, Calico)
   - `az acr login --name <acr>` ÔÇö verify ACR accessible
   - `kubectl run test --image=<acr>.azurecr.io/hello-world --rm -it` ÔÇö verify AKS can pull from ACR
5. **Node failure resilience (manual):** `kubectl drain <node> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-emptydir-data` ÔÇö verify pods reschedule, no data loss
6. **Autoscaler verification:** Deploy enough pods to trigger scale-up, verify nodes added (3 Ôćĺ 4+)
7. **State verification:** Confirm state file path in Azure Storage

### Key Gotchas & Warnings

1. **Network policy is IMMUTABLE.** Setting `network_policy = "calico"` (or `"azure"`) at cluster creation is permanent. If omitted, NetworkPolicies will NOT work in Story 1.3. This is the #1 gotcha for this story.
2. **Workload Identity requires OIDC.** Both `oidc_issuer_enabled` and `workload_identity_enabled` must be `true`. Missing either blocks Story 3.1 (Key Vault) and Story 5.1 (Monitoring).
3. **System node pool cannot be deleted.** The default node pool (system) is permanent. Choose VM size carefully ÔÇö it cannot be changed later without recreating the cluster.
4. **ACR naming:** No hyphens allowed. Max 50 chars. Must be globally unique.
5. **Subnet sizing for Azure CNI:** Each pod gets a VNet IP. With `/24` subnets (251 IPs) and `max_pods = 50` per node, you can support ~5 nodes per subnet. The `/24` subnets from Story 1.1 are adequate for 3-10 node autoscaling (system pool uses `snet-aks-system`, user pool uses `snet-aks-user`).
6. **Do NOT create namespaces in this story.** Namespace provisioning is Story 1.3.
7. **Do NOT install Helm charts (monitoring, ArgoCD) in this story.** Those are separate stories (4.4, 5.1).
8. **Do NOT configure RBAC/AD group mappings.** That is Story 1.3.
9. **Brazil South availability zones:** All 3 zones (1, 2, 3) ARE supported. Use `availability_zones = ["1", "2", "3"]` for HA.
10. **ACR Premium SKU cost:** Premium is recommended for production (geo-replication, content trust, private endpoints). For DEV, `Standard` SKU may suffice to save cost. Consider making SKU configurable per environment.
11. **March 31, 2026 outbound access change:** Azure removes default outbound access for new VMs. Ensure `outbound_type = "loadBalancer"` is explicitly set in `network_profile`. Our existing VNet (Story 1.1) should be safe but verify.
12. **ACR role assignment propagation:** Permissions take 5-10 minutes to propagate. If pods fail to pull images immediately after `terraform apply`, wait and retry ÔÇö this is normal.

- `terraform/backend.tf` ÔÇö Updated key from `networking/terraform.tfstate` to `terraform.tfstate` (MODIFIED)
- `terraform/environments/dev.tfvars` ÔÇö Added AKS values: Free tier, Standard_D4s_v3/v5, acrhyperadev, Standard ACR (MODIFIED)
- `terraform/environments/prd.tfvars` ÔÇö Added AKS values: Standard tier, Standard_D4s_v3/v5, acrhyperaprd, Premium ACR (MODIFIED)
- `terraform/scripts/aks-health-check.sh` ÔÇö Post-deploy health check: cluster info, nodes, system pods, ACR access (NEW)

    17Ôćĺ---
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ# Architecture Decision Document
    20Ôćĺ
    21Ôćĺ_This document builds collaboratively through step-by-step discovery. Sections are appended as we work through each architectural decision together._
    22Ôćĺ
    23Ôćĺ## Project Context Analysis
    24Ôćĺ
    25Ôćĺ### Requirements Overview
    26Ôćĺ
    27Ôćĺ**Functional Requirements:**
    28Ôćĺ47 FRs across 9 capability groups. The architecture must support two distinct planes:
    29Ôćĺ1. **Developer Plane** ÔÇö boilerplate template, code push, PR workflow, credential references (FR1-6, FR7-13)
    30Ôćĺ2. **Platform Plane** ÔÇö build pipeline, deployment, scheduling, monitoring, cost tracking, alerting, access control, infrastructure provisioning (FR14-47)
    31Ôćĺ
    32ÔćĺThese planes interact at well-defined boundaries: the PR merge triggers the build pipeline, the boilerplate template defines the contract between developer code and platform services, and namespace isolation separates business areas.
    33Ôćĺ
    34Ôćĺ**Non-Functional Requirements:**
    35Ôćĺ26 NFRs that impose specific architectural constraints:
    36Ôćĺ- **Security (NFR1-7):** Zero-trust credential model, namespace network isolation, RBAC verification, container vulnerability scanning, SSO-only access
    37Ôćĺ- **Reliability (NFR8-13):** 99.5% execution availability, auto-retry with alerting, no SPOF, ArgoCD drift self-heal, secret rotation resilience
    38Ôćĺ- **Scalability (NFR14-17):** 5Ôćĺ60+ CronJobs growth path, node autoscaling 3-10, 20 concurrent jobs per namespace
    39Ôćĺ- **Performance (NFR18-21):** <60s pod startup (cached), <3s dashboard load, <10min CI pipeline, <5s log queries
    40Ôćĺ- **Integration (NFR22-26):** SAP connectivity verification, <5min ArgoCD sync, <60s alert delivery, 99% CI trigger reliability, 30s metrics scrape interval
    41Ôćĺ
    42Ôćĺ**Scale & Complexity:**
    43Ôćĺ
    44Ôćĺ- Primary domain: Infrastructure / Platform Engineering
    45Ôćĺ- Complexity level: High
    46Ôćĺ- Estimated architectural components: 12-15 (AKS cluster, ArgoCD, CI pipelines, ACR, Key Vault + CSI driver, Robusta (Prometheus + Grafana + AlertManager + alert enrichment), LGTM backends (Mimir, Loki, Tempo), Teams alerting via Robusta sinks, namespace provisioning, boilerplate template, base Docker images, network policies, RBAC configuration)
    47Ôćĺ
    48Ôćĺ### Technical Constraints & Dependencies
    49Ôćĺ
    50Ôćĺ- **Azure-only cloud** ÔÇö corporate standard, no multi-cloud consideration needed
    51Ôćĺ- **Kubernetes CronJobs only** ÔÇö no long-running services, no event-driven execution for MVP
    52Ôćĺ- **Azure DevOps for source control and CI** ÔÇö not GitHub, not GitLab
    53Ôćĺ- **ArgoCD for GitOps CD** ÔÇö already decided, not evaluating alternatives
    54Ôćĺ- **SAP on-prem/private network** ÔÇö requires VNet integration or peering from AKS to SAP endpoints
    55Ôćĺ- **AD group-based identity** ÔÇö 4 AD groups (DEV, REVIEW, OPS, ADMIN) map to RBAC roles
    56Ôćĺ- **Two environments only for MVP** ÔÇö DEV + PRD (QA deferred to Phase 2)
    57Ôćĺ- **Python 3.13** ÔÇö runtime version locked
    58Ôćĺ- **Team size: 3 core** ÔÇö Juliano (infra), Christian (K8s), Ricardo (CI/CD)
    59Ôćĺ
    60Ôćĺ### Cross-Cutting Concerns Identified
    61Ôćĺ
    62Ôćĺ1. **Secret Lifecycle** ÔÇö Key Vault provisioning Ôćĺ CSI driver injection Ôćĺ namespace scoping Ôćĺ rotation resilience Ôćĺ audit logging. Touches FR21-24, NFR1-3, NFR13.
    63Ôćĺ2. **Namespace-as-Tenant** ÔÇö provisioning Ôćĺ RBAC binding Ôćĺ resource quotas Ôćĺ network isolation Ôćĺ ArgoCD app targeting Ôćĺ cost attribution. Touches FR39-43, FR20, NFR5-6, NFR16, FR31-34.
    64Ôćĺ3. **Observability Chain** ÔÇö Robusta (embedded kube-prometheus-stack) Ôćĺ Prometheus remote-write Ôćĺ Mimir (long-term metrics) + Loki (logs) + Tempo (traces) Ôćĺ Grafana dashboards Ôćĺ alert enrichment + playbooks Ôćĺ Teams/Email sinks Ôćĺ KRR cost recommendations. Touches FR25-38, NFR10, NFR19, NFR21, NFR24, NFR26.
    65Ôćĺ4. **Developer Contract (Boilerplate)** ÔÇö template structure Ôćĺ Dockerfile Ôćĺ base image selection Ôćĺ src/ boundary Ôćĺ config Ôćĺ README requirements. Touches FR1-6, FR13.
    66Ôćĺ5. **GitOps Pipeline** ÔÇö Azure DevOps PR Ôćĺ CI build + scan Ôćĺ ACR push Ôćĺ ArgoCD sync Ôćĺ CronJob update. Touches FR7-15, NFR4, NFR20, NFR23, NFR25.
    67Ôćĺ
    68Ôćĺ## Starter Template Evaluation
    69Ôćĺ
    70Ôćĺ### Primary Technology Domain
    71Ôćĺ
    72ÔćĺInfrastructure / Platform Engineering ÔÇö Terraform + Kubernetes (Helm) platform with Python CronJob workloads on Azure AKS.
    73Ôćĺ
    74Ôćĺ### Starter Options Considered
    75Ôćĺ
    76ÔćĺThis project is not a typical application with web framework starters. It is an infrastructure platform built with Terraform modules, Helm charts, and GitOps manifests. The "starter" is the project structure and tooling choices themselves.
    77Ôćĺ
    78Ôćĺ**Option 1: Monorepo Terraform + K8s Manifests (Selected)**
    79Ôćĺ- Single repository with `terraform/` for IaC and `k8s/` for Kubernetes manifests
    80Ôćĺ- Terraform modules deploy Helm charts via `helm_release` provider
    81Ôćĺ- ArgoCD watches `k8s/` directory for GitOps reconciliation
    82Ôćĺ- Aligns with existing `docs/source-tree-analysis.md` structure
    83Ôćĺ
    84Ôćĺ**Option 2: Separate Repos (Rejected)**
    85Ôćĺ- Terraform in one repo, K8s manifests in another
    86Ôćĺ- Rejected: team of 3 doesn't benefit from multi-repo overhead; single repo simplifies cross-cutting changes
    87Ôćĺ
    88Ôćĺ### Selected Approach: Monorepo with Terraform Modules + Helm Values
    89Ôćĺ
    90Ôćĺ**Rationale:** Matches existing project structure, team size, and the platform engineering domain where infrastructure and manifests are tightly coupled.
    91Ôćĺ
    92Ôćĺ### Project Structure
    93Ôćĺ
    94Ôćĺ```
    95Ôćĺterraform/
    96ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ modules/
    97ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ aks/                      # AKS cluster provisioning
    98ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ monitoring/               # LGTM stack + Robusta deployment via Helm provider
    99ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ main.tf              # helm_release for Robusta, Mimir, Loki, Tempo
   100ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ storage.tf           # Azure Storage Account + 3 containers
   101ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ identity.tf          # User-Assigned Managed Identity (workload identity)
   102ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ variables.tf
   103ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ outputs.tf
   104ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ argocd/                   # ArgoCD deployment + ApplicationSets
   105ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ namespaces/               # Namespace provisioning + RBAC + quotas
   106ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ networking/               # VNet, peering, network policies
   107ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ security/                 # Key Vault, CSI driver, secret classes
   108ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ ci/                       # ACR, service connections, build pipelines
   109Ôćĺ
   110Ôćĺk8s/
   111ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ platform/
   112ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ monitoring/
   113ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ robusta-values.yaml   # Robusta Helm values (Prometheus remote-write to Mimir)
   114ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ mimir-values.yaml     # Mimir distributed values (Azure Blob: mimir-blocks)
   115ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ loki-values.yaml      # Loki values (Azure Blob: loki-chunks)
   116ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ tempo-values.yaml     # Tempo distributed values (Azure Blob: tempo-traces)
   117ÔćĺÔöé   Ôöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ dashboards/           # Custom Grafana dashboard JSONs
   118ÔćĺÔöé   ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ argocd/
   119ÔćĺÔöé   ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ base-configs/
   120ÔćĺÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ apps/                          # ArgoCD Application manifests per namespace
   121ÔćĺÔööÔöÇÔöÇ boilerplate/                   # CronJob template for developers
   122Ôćĺ```
   123Ôćĺ
   124Ôćĺ### Version Table
   125Ôćĺ
   126Ôćĺ| Component | Version | Source |
   127Ôćĺ|-----------|---------|--------|
   128Ôćĺ| Terraform | >= 1.9 | hashicorp.com |
   129Ôćĺ| AzureRM Provider | ~> 4.x | registry.terraform.io |
   130Ôćĺ| Helm Provider | ~> 2.x | registry.terraform.io |
   131Ôćĺ| Robusta | 0.10.30+ | robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com |
   132Ôćĺ| Mimir (distributed) | latest | grafana.github.io/helm-charts |
   133Ôćĺ| Loki | 6.53.0 | grafana.github.io/helm-charts |
   134Ôćĺ| Tempo (distributed) | latest | grafana.github.io/helm-charts |
   135Ôćĺ| ArgoCD | latest stable | argoproj.github.io/argo-helm |
   136Ôćĺ| Python | 3.13 | base Docker images |
   137Ôćĺ
   138Ôćĺ### Azure Storage for LGTM Backends
   139Ôćĺ
   140ÔćĺThe `terraform/modules/monitoring/storage.tf` provisions:
   141Ôćĺ
   142Ôćĺ| Resource | Purpose |
   143Ôćĺ|----------|---------|
   144Ôćĺ| Storage Account (`stlgtm<env>`) | Shared account for all LGTM backends |
   145Ôćĺ| Container: `mimir-blocks` | Mimir TSDB blocks (long-term metrics) |
   146Ôćĺ| Container: `loki-chunks` | Loki log chunks + index |
   147Ôćĺ| Container: `tempo-traces` | Tempo trace data |
   148Ôćĺ| User-Assigned Managed Identity | Workload identity for pod Ôćĺ storage auth |
   149Ôćĺ
   150Ôćĺ### PRD Requirement Mapping
   151Ôćĺ
   152Ôćĺ| Requirement | How Architecture Satisfies |
   153Ôćĺ|-------------|---------------------------|
   154Ôćĺ| FR1-6 (Boilerplate) | `k8s/boilerplate/` template with Dockerfile, src/ boundary, config |
   155Ôćĺ| FR7-13 (CI/CD) | Azure DevOps pipelines Ôćĺ ACR Ôćĺ ArgoCD sync |
   156Ôćĺ| FR14-24 (Platform) | Terraform modules for AKS, namespaces, Key Vault, networking |
   157Ôćĺ| FR25-30 (Monitoring) | Robusta + Mimir (365d long-term metrics retention) |
   158Ôćĺ| FR31-34 (Cost Tracking) | Mimir historical metrics + KRR cost recommendations |
   159Ôćĺ| FR35-38 (Alerting) | Robusta alert enrichment Ôćĺ Teams/Email sinks |
   160Ôćĺ| FR39-47 (Access/Infra) | Terraform namespace module with RBAC, quotas, network policies |
   161Ôćĺ| NFR19 (<3s dashboard) | Mimir query frontend + caching |
   162Ôćĺ| NFR21 (<5s log queries) | Loki TSDB schema v13 + index gateway |
   163Ôćĺ| New: Distributed Tracing | Tempo enables CronJob execution tracing via OpenTelemetry |
   164Ôćĺ
   165Ôćĺ### Data Flow ÔÇö Observability
   166Ôćĺ
   167Ôćĺ```
   168ÔćĺCronJob pods
   169Ôćĺ  ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ metrics Ôćĺ Prometheus (Robusta) Ôćĺ remote-write Ôćĺ Mimir (Azure Blob)
   170Ôćĺ  ÔöťÔöÇÔöÇ logs Ôćĺ Loki (Azure Blob)
   171Ôćĺ  ÔööÔöÇÔöÇ traces Ôćĺ Tempo (Azure Blob) [via OpenTelemetry]
   172Ôćĺ                    Ôćô
   173Ôćĺ              Grafana (Robusta embedded)
   174Ôćĺ              connects to all 3 backends
   175Ôćĺ                    Ôćô
   176Ôćĺ              Robusta alert enrichment Ôćĺ Teams/Email sinks
   177Ôćĺ```
   178Ôćĺ
   179Ôćĺ**Note:** Single Grafana instance (embedded in Robusta's kube-prometheus-stack) connects to Mimir, Loki, and Tempo as data sources. No duplicate Grafana deployment.
   180Ôćĺ
   181Ôćĺ## Core Architectural Decisions
   182Ôćĺ
   183Ôćĺ### Decision Priority Analysis
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ**Critical Decisions (Block Implementation):**
   186Ôćĺ1. Terraform state backend and segmentation
   187Ôćĺ2. Secret management pattern (Key Vault Ôćĺ CSI Driver)
   188Ôćĺ3. SAP connectivity pattern
   189Ôćĺ4. ArgoCD application management model
   190Ôćĺ5. Network policy model
   191Ôćĺ
   192Ôćĺ**Important Decisions (Shape Architecture):**
   193Ôćĺ6. Container vulnerability scanning
   194Ôćĺ7. Image tagging and promotion strategy
   195Ôćĺ8. OpenTelemetry instrumentation approach
   196Ôćĺ9. Log format standardization
   197Ôćĺ10. Alert routing rules
   198Ôćĺ
   199Ôćĺ**Deferred Decisions (Post-MVP):**
   200Ôćĺ- Multi-cluster federation (Phase 2 with QA environment)
   201Ôćĺ- Service mesh consideration (not needed for CronJobs)
   202Ôćĺ- Advanced cost showback per-namespace (Phase 2)
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ### Category 1: State & Storage
   205Ôćĺ
   206Ôćĺ**Decision 1.1: Terraform State Backend**
   207Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Azure Storage Account with blob backend
   208Ôćĺ- **Version:** AzureRM Provider ~> 4.60
   209Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** Azure-only constraint makes Azure Storage the natural choice. No Terraform Cloud dependency, keeps everything within corporate Azure tenant. State encryption at rest via Azure Storage SSE.
   210Ôćĺ- **Configuration:**
   211Ôćĺ  - Storage Account: `sttfstate<env><region>` with RA-GRS replication
   212Ôćĺ  - Container: `tfstate`
   213Ôćĺ  - State locking: Azure Blob lease (built-in with azurerm backend)
   214Ôćĺ  - Access: Managed Identity for CI/CD service connections
   215Ôćĺ
   216Ôćĺ**Decision 1.2: State File Segmentation**
   217Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Per-module state files with shared networking/identity state
   218Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** Team of 3 needs blast radius isolation. A failed monitoring deploy must not block namespace provisioning. Per-module state allows parallel `terraform apply` across modules.
   219Ôćĺ- **State Keys:**
   220Ôćĺ  - `networking/terraform.tfstate` ÔÇö VNet, subnets, peering
   221Ôćĺ  - `aks/terraform.tfstate` ÔÇö AKS cluster, node pools
   222Ôćĺ  - `security/terraform.tfstate` ÔÇö Key Vault, CSI driver, managed identities
   223Ôćĺ  - `monitoring/terraform.tfstate` ÔÇö LGTM stack, Robusta, storage
   224Ôćĺ  - `argocd/terraform.tfstate` ÔÇö ArgoCD deployment
   225Ôćĺ  - `namespaces/terraform.tfstate` ÔÇö Namespace provisioning, RBAC, quotas
   226Ôćĺ  - `ci/terraform.tfstate` ÔÇö ACR, build pipelines, service connections
   227Ôćĺ
   228Ôćĺ### Category 2: Secret Management & Zero-Trust
   229Ôćĺ
   230Ôćĺ**Decision 2.1: Key Vault Ôćĺ CSI Driver Pattern**
   231Ôćĺ- **Choice:** One Key Vault per environment, SecretProviderClass per namespace
   232Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** Single Key Vault simplifies management for team of 3. Namespace-scoped SecretProviderClass resources ensure tenant isolation. CSI driver mounts secrets as volumes ÔÇö no Kubernetes Secret objects stored in etcd.
   233Ôćĺ- **Pattern:**
   234Ôćĺ  - Key Vault: `kv-hypera-<env>` (DEV, PRD)
   235Ôćĺ  - Access: User-Assigned Managed Identity per namespace (workload identity)
   236Ôćĺ  - SecretProviderClass: one per namespace, scoped to that namespace's secrets
   237Ôćĺ  - Sync frequency: `rotationPollInterval: 2m` (CSI driver default)
   238Ôćĺ
   239Ôćĺ**Decision 2.2: Network Policy Model**
   240Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Default-deny per namespace with explicit allowlists
   241Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** NFR5 requires namespace network isolation. Default-deny ensures new namespaces are isolated by default. Explicit NetworkPolicy resources allow only required egress (SAP endpoints, Azure services, DNS).
   242Ôćĺ- **Implementation:** Calico or Azure NPM (Azure CNI native). Terraform namespace module provisions default-deny + standard egress rules.
   243Ôćĺ
   244Ôćĺ**Decision 2.3: Container Vulnerability Scanning**
   245Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Trivy Operator (continuous in-cluster scanning)
   246Ôćĺ- **Version:** Trivy v0.69.x / Trivy Operator latest
   247Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** Open-source, Kubernetes-native, scans images + IaC + RBAC. Results as CRDs queryable via kubectl. CI pipeline also runs `trivy image` pre-push to ACR. Complements (not replaces) Microsoft Defender for Containers if corporate mandates it.
   248Ôćĺ- **Affects:** CI pipeline definition, ACR push gate, continuous cluster scanning
   249Ôćĺ
   250Ôćĺ### Category 3: Service Connectivity
   251Ôćĺ
   252Ôćĺ**Decision 3.1: SAP Connectivity Pattern**
   253Ôćĺ- **Choice:** VNet peering from AKS VNet to SAP private network
   254Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** FR22 requires SAP on-prem/private connectivity. VNet peering provides lowest latency, no data transit costs within region, and uses existing corporate network topology. Private DNS zones resolve SAP hostnames within the peered network.
   255Ôćĺ- **Implementation:**
   256Ôćĺ  - Terraform `azurerm_virtual_network_peering` in networking module
   257Ôćĺ  - Private DNS zone for SAP endpoint resolution
   258Ôćĺ  - Network policies allow egress to SAP CIDR only from authorized namespaces
   259Ôćĺ
   260Ôćĺ**Decision 3.2: CronJob Ôćĺ External Service Auth**
   261Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Workload Identity (User-Assigned Managed Identity per namespace)
   262Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** Zero-trust model ÔÇö no static credentials. Each namespace's managed identity is scoped to only the Azure resources and SAP endpoints that namespace's CronJobs need. Key Vault stores any non-Azure credentials (SAP RFC passwords) injected via CSI driver.
   263Ôćĺ- **Pattern:** Pod Ôćĺ Workload Identity Ôćĺ Azure AD token Ôćĺ Azure resource / Key Vault Ôćĺ SAP credential
   264Ôćĺ
   265Ôćĺ**Decision 3.3: DNS Resolution**
   266Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Azure Private DNS Zones linked to AKS VNet
   267Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** CronJobs need to resolve SAP hostnames, Key Vault endpoints, and Storage Account endpoints without public DNS exposure. CoreDNS in AKS forwards to Azure DNS which resolves private zones.
   268Ôćĺ
   269Ôćĺ### Category 4: GitOps & Pipeline
   270Ôćĺ
   271Ôćĺ**Decision 4.1: ArgoCD Application Management**
   272Ôćĺ- **Choice:** ApplicationSets with Git generator per namespace
   273Ôćĺ- **Version:** ArgoCD Helm chart v9.4.x
   274Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** ApplicationSets auto-generate ArgoCD Applications from directory structure. When a new namespace directory appears in `k8s/apps/<namespace>/`, ArgoCD automatically creates the Application. Scales from 5 to 60+ CronJobs without manual Application creation.
   275Ôćĺ- **Pattern:**
   276Ôćĺ  ```yaml
   277Ôćĺ  # ApplicationSet watches k8s/apps/*/
   278Ôćĺ  generators:
   279Ôćĺ    - git:
   280Ôćĺ        repoURL: <azure-devops-repo>
   281Ôćĺ        directories:
   282Ôćĺ          - path: k8s/apps/*
   283Ôćĺ  ```
   284Ôćĺ
   285Ôćĺ**Decision 4.2: Image Tagging Strategy**
   286Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Git SHA tags for traceability + `latest-<env>` mutable tags for ArgoCD
   287Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** Git SHA provides immutable audit trail (NFR1). Mutable `latest-dev` / `latest-prd` tags let ArgoCD Image Updater auto-sync. CI pipeline tags both: `acr.azurecr.io/app:abc1234` + `acr.azurecr.io/app:latest-dev`.
   288Ôćĺ- **Affects:** CI pipeline, ArgoCD Image Updater config, ACR retention policy
   289Ôćĺ
   290Ôćĺ**Decision 4.3: Promotion Model DEV Ôćĺ PRD**
   291Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Manual approval gate via Azure DevOps Release Pipeline
   292Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** Team of 3, high-consequence workloads (SAP integrations). Automated promotion is premature for MVP. PR to `prd` branch triggers pipeline with manual approval stage. ArgoCD syncs PRD cluster from `prd` branch.
   293Ôćĺ- **Flow:** Dev branch merge Ôćĺ CI builds Ôćĺ DEV auto-deploy Ôćĺ Manual approval Ôćĺ PRD branch merge Ôćĺ PRD auto-deploy
   294Ôćĺ
   295Ôćĺ**Decision 4.4: Base Docker Image Management**
   296Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Shared base images in ACR, maintained by infra team
   297Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** Python 3.13 locked. Single `base-python:3.13-slim` image with common dependencies (Azure SDK, SAP libraries). Developers' Dockerfiles `FROM` this base. Trivy scans base image on schedule. Reduces per-CronJob image build time and ensures consistent security baseline.
   298Ôćĺ
   299Ôćĺ### Category 5: Observability Decisions
   300Ôćĺ
   301Ôćĺ**Decision 5.1: OpenTelemetry Instrumentation**
   302Ôćĺ- **Choice:** OTel Operator auto-instrumentation for Python
   303Ôćĺ- **Version:** OpenTelemetry Operator latest, autoinstrumentation-python 0.39b0+
   304Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** Zero-code instrumentation via pod annotations. OTel Operator injects Python auto-instrumentation at pod startup. No developer code changes required. Traces sent to Tempo via OTel Collector (HTTP port 4318).
   305Ôćĺ- **Pattern:**
   306Ôćĺ  ```yaml
   307Ôćĺ  # Instrumentation CR in each namespace
   308Ôćĺ  apiVersion: opentelemetry.io/v1alpha1
   309Ôćĺ  kind: Instrumentation
   310Ôćĺ  metadata:
   311Ôćĺ    name: python-instrumentation
   312Ôćĺ  spec:
   313Ôćĺ    exporter:
   314Ôćĺ      endpoint: http://tempo-distributor.monitoring:4318
   315Ôćĺ    python:
   316Ôćĺ      image: ghcr.io/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator/autoinstrumentation-python:latest
   317Ôćĺ  ```
   318Ôćĺ- **Pod annotation:** `instrumentation.opentelemetry.io/inject-python: "true"`
   319Ôćĺ
   320Ôćĺ**Decision 5.2: Log Format Standardization**
   321Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Structured JSON logs with standard fields
   322Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** NFR21 requires <5s log queries. Structured JSON enables Loki to parse and index fields without regex. Boilerplate template includes Python `structlog` configuration with standard fields: `timestamp`, `level`, `message`, `cronjob_name`, `namespace`, `trace_id`.
   323Ôćĺ- **Standard Fields:**
   324Ôćĺ  - `timestamp` (ISO 8601)
   325Ôćĺ  - `level` (INFO, WARNING, ERROR)
   326Ôćĺ  - `message`
   327Ôćĺ  - `cronjob_name`
   328Ôćĺ  - `namespace`
   329Ôćĺ  - `trace_id` (correlated with Tempo)
   330Ôćĺ  - `run_id` (unique per CronJob execution)
   331Ôćĺ
   332Ôćĺ**Decision 5.3: Alert Routing Rules**
   333Ôćĺ- **Choice:** Severity-based routing via Robusta sinks
   334Ôćĺ- **Rationale:** NFR24 requires <60s alert delivery. Robusta playbooks enrich alerts with pod logs, resource usage, and suggested fixes. Routing by severity ensures the right people get the right alerts.
   335Ôćĺ- **Routing:**
   336Ôćĺ  | Severity | Channel | Response |
   337Ôćĺ  |----------|---------|----------|
   338Ôćĺ  | Critical | Teams (OPS channel) + Email to on-call | Immediate |
   339Ôćĺ  | Warning | Teams (OPS channel) | Business hours |
   340Ôćĺ  | Info | Grafana dashboard only | Review weekly |
   341Ôćĺ- **Affects:** Robusta playbooks, Teams webhook configuration, email sink
   342Ôćĺ
   343Ôćĺ### Decision Impact Analysis
   344Ôćĺ
   345Ôćĺ**Implementation Sequence:**
   346Ôćĺ1. Networking (VNet, peering, DNS) ÔÇö foundation for everything
   347Ôćĺ2. AKS cluster ÔÇö requires networking
   348Ôćĺ3. Security (Key Vault, CSI driver, managed identities) ÔÇö requires AKS
   349Ôćĺ4. Monitoring (LGTM + Robusta + OTel Operator) ÔÇö requires AKS + storage
   350Ôćĺ5. ArgoCD ÔÇö requires AKS + networking
   351Ôćĺ6. Namespaces (RBAC, quotas, network policies, SecretProviderClass) ÔÇö requires all above
   352Ôćĺ7. CI (ACR, pipelines, base images) ÔÇö requires AKS + ACR
   353Ôćĺ8. Boilerplate template ÔÇö requires all platform services running
   354Ôćĺ
   355Ôćĺ**Cross-Component Dependencies:**
   356Ôćĺ- Workload Identity Ôćĺ used by LGTM pods (storage access), CronJob pods (Key Vault, SAP), ArgoCD (repo access)
   357Ôćĺ- VNet peering Ôćĺ required by SAP connectivity, Private DNS zones, Key Vault private endpoints
   358Ôćĺ- Trivy Ôćĺ scans base images (ACR), cluster resources (AKS), and Helm charts (ArgoCD)
   359Ôćĺ- OTel Operator Ôćĺ depends on Tempo being deployed first (Instrumentation CR references Tempo endpoint)
   360Ôćĺ- Structured logging Ôćĺ boilerplate template must include structlog config that correlates with Tempo trace_id
   361Ôćĺ
   362Ôćĺ### Updated Version Table
   363Ôćĺ
   364Ôćĺ| Component | Version | Source |
   365Ôćĺ|-----------|---------|--------|
   366Ôćĺ| Terraform | >= 1.14 | [hashicorp.com](https://releases.hashicorp.com/terraform/) |
   367Ôćĺ| AzureRM Provider | ~> 4.60 | [registry.terraform.io](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/azurerm/latest) |
   368Ôćĺ| Helm Provider | ~> 2.x | registry.terraform.io |
   369Ôćĺ| Robusta | 0.10.30+ | robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com |
   370Ôćĺ| Mimir (distributed) | latest (v4.1+) | [grafana.github.io/helm-charts](https://grafana.com/docs/helm-charts/mimir-distributed/latest/) |
   371Ôćĺ| Loki | 6.53.0 | grafana.github.io/helm-charts |
   372Ôćĺ| Tempo (distributed) | 1.61.x | [grafana.github.io/helm-charts](https://artifacthub.io/packages/helm/grafana/tempo-distributed) |
   373Ôćĺ| ArgoCD | Helm chart v9.4.x | [argoproj.github.io/argo-helm](https://artifacthub.io/packages/helm/argo/argo-cd) |
   374Ôćĺ| Trivy Operator | v0.69.x | [aquasecurity/trivy](https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy) |
   375Ôćĺ| OTel Operator | latest | [open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-operator) |
   376Ôćĺ| Python | 3.13 | base Docker images |
   377Ôćĺ
